items.text:
Extended Hours for the Mitchell Hall Makerspace

The Department of Engineering is pleased to announce that the Mitchell Hall Makerspace will extend its operating hours beginning Monday, November 3. The space will now be open from 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM on weekdays and from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM on Saturdays.

All currently enrolled students may use the 3D printers, laser cutters, and soldering stations at no charge. However, please note the following guidelines:

- A refundable deposit of $20.00 is required before borrowing any handheld power tools.
- Materials such as filament and acrylic sheets must be purchased at the front desk in Room 112.
- Students must complete the online safety tutorial before their first visit.

Staff members will be available throughout the day to assist with equipment and answer questions. Reservations for the laser cutters are strongly recommended, as demand increases near the end of the semester. To reserve a station or report a problem, contact the Makerspace office at makerspace@university.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do before their first visit to the makerspace?

options.A: To pay a refundable equipment deposit
options.B: To complete the online safety tutorial
options.C: To reserve a laser cutter in advance
options.D: To purchase filament from the front desk

questions.explanation: The notice states that students must complete the online safety tutorial before their first visit. The deposit applies only to borrowing power tools, reservations are merely recommended, and buying filament is a general rule, not a first-visit requirement.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about reserving a laser cutter near the end of the semester?

options.A: It requires a separate borrowing deposit
options.B: It costs an extra twenty-dollar fee
options.C: It may be difficult without a reservation
options.D: It is arranged at the front desk instead

questions.explanation: The notice says reservations are strongly recommended because demand increases near the end of the semester, implying that getting a station without one becomes harder. The deposit and fee distractors reuse passage numbers in the wrong context, and the front-desk option misattributes the reservation process.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce extended hours and new usage rules
options.B: To advertise a new engineering degree program
options.C: To request donations for new lab equipment
options.D: To explain how to repair the 3D printers

questions.explanation: The notice announces the makerspace's extended operating hours and then lists the guidelines for using it, so its overall purpose is to inform students of the new hours and rules. The other options describe topics the notice never addresses.
